item.title: Netflix expands revamped mobile app across Asia and doubles down on kids’ gaming
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: The media giant is pushing to expand its mobile and gaming business.

item.title: ‘AI-pilled’ firms spend $7,500 per employee each month on AI
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: The most AI-obsessed firms are spending roughly $7,500 monthly per employee on AI, per Ramp AI Index. That's not more than an engineer's salary — yet.

item.title: The Indian government got cold feet on Starlink just before SpaceX’s IPO
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Problems with Starlink's India expansion could challenge SpaceX's IPO growth story.

item.title: How memory tools can make AI models worse
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: New research suggests that AI memory systems can degrade model performance and encourage sycophantic tendencies.

item.title: Zest launches a restaurant discovery app powered by where people actually eat
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Backed by Alexis Ohanian’s 776 and Kindred Ventures, Zest uses transaction data and AI to generate restaurant recommendations based on users’ real dining habits and the places they frequent.

item.title: Cybersecurity researchers aren’t happy about the guardrails on Anthropic’s Fable
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are complaining that Anthropic's new model Fable has guardrails that are too strict for any cybersecurity work.

item.title: Why enterprise AI will be a major focus at VivaTech 2026
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: While Silicon Valley continues pushing aggressively into large language models and consumer-facing AI products, many European companies are focused on applying AI to complex systems already embedded into everyday life.

item.title: Datadog veterans launch AI coding startup Niteshift on a bet against Big AI lock-in
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: AI coding agent startup Niteshift has raised a $7 million seed round from a who's who of angels. It's betting companies will want power over, not lock-in with model makers.

item.title: The three hard-tech moonshots fueling SpaceX’s unbelievable IPO
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Most of the value in SpaceX's IPO is effectively a call option on the company's ambitious space data center plans.

item.title: Pinterest bets on creators with Amazon Storefront integration
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Pinterest is adding support for Amazon Storefronts, allowing creators to earn affiliate commissions more easily while showcasing their product recommendations in one place.

item.title: Warner Music acquires AI attribution startup Sureel AI
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Through the acquisition, WMG aims to better track when its artists' work is used in AI-generated content or for training AI models.

item.title: ServiceNow tells customers a bug left some of their data exposed to the internet
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: ServiceNow is used by thousands of enterprises to automate their internal processes, but says several customers had data accessed because of a security bug.

item.title: Jedify raises $24M to help companies arm AI agents with context on their business
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: The funding round was led by Norwest, with participation from S Capital VC, Cerca Partners, and Oceans Ventures. Snowflake Ventures also participated as a strategic investor.

item.title: Decart’s new world model can simulate hours of photorealistic driving — with some caveats
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Decart is launching Oasis 3, a real-time world model that generates photorealistic driving environments for autonomous vehicle testing, now available via API for developers to build on.

item.title: Snapchat limits users under 16 to sharing Spotlights with friends
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Users under 16 years old will get a separate profile to show Stories and Spotlight posts to friends that they follow back.

item.title: Why everyone’s an energy company now
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Electricity demand from AI data centers is pushing everyone — including automakers like GM and Ford — into the energy storage business.

item.title: Avalanche’s desktop fusion reactor delivers blistering-hot plasma
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Fusion power startup Avalanche Energy said its reactor prototype heated a plasma to over 10 million degrees Celsius.

item.title: Why 2 SpaceX alumni are betting on solar and batteries to power the AI craze
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Ambrosia Energy wants to build power plants in less than 12 months while undercutting natural gas. It hopes to build gigawatts' worth by 2030.

item.title: Waymo says it built a better benchmark for comparing robotaxis to humans
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: Waymo created a new computer model to help it better understand how humans behave in crash scenarios that its robotaxis encounter.

item.title: Meta signs first AI data center deal in India with Reliance
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: The 168-megawatt facility will support Meta's global AI computing needs and can be expanded over time.
